news.source: ABC News & Headlines – Australian Broadcasting Corporation

sections.heading: Seruvakula bolsters pack for Liverpool test
sections.body: Interim coach Senirusi Seruvakula has injected significant experience into the starting XV, with Peceli Yato returning to the blindside and Levani Botia moving to No. 8. Mesake Doge reclaims the tighthead jersey, while Caleb Muntz is preferred at fly-half to dictate the tempo. The squad is looking to pair their natural attacking flair with greater tactical discipline after their opening loss.

sections.heading: Squad remains available for selection
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news in today's coverage regarding the matchday squad. Following the disappointment in Cardiff, coaching staff have confirmed that the players are in good health and have responded with high energy in training this week. The coaching group has expressed that the squad has recovered well and is fully prepared for the challenges posed by the England pack.

sections.heading: Borthwick rings changes under mounting pressure
sections.body: England head into this clash desperate to snap a five-match losing streak, with Steve Borthwick recalling Henry Slade to the midfield. Tommy Freeman shifts to the wing to accommodate the change, while Guy Pepper starts at openside flanker. The hosts have also named three debutants on the bench—Benhard Janse van Rensburg, Noah Caluori, and George Kloska—as they seek a necessary response in Liverpool.

sections.heading: Seeking a vital bounce-back result
sections.body: Both sides enter this encounter aiming to register their first points of the Nations Championship following opening-round defeats. For the Flying Fijians, a positive result at the Hill Dickinson Stadium would be a significant statement against Tier One opposition. As Seruvakula noted, the team has identified crucial lessons in discipline and decision-making from last weekend that must be applied to secure a victory.

sections.heading: Slade starts as Borthwick looks for response
sections.body: Steve Borthwick has handed a starting berth to Henry Slade, who returns to the midfield for today’s encounter. Tommy Freeman shifts to the wing following the injury to Cadan Murley, while Guy Pepper earns a start at openside flanker. Three players—Benhard Janse van Rensburg, Noah Caluori, and George Kloska—are set to make their international debuts should they appear from the bench.

sections.heading: Murley ruled out of Saturday's Test
sections.body: Cadan Murley is unavailable for selection today after sustaining an injury during the defeat to South Africa. The camp has not reported further fresh fitness concerns following the opening round. George Furbank, who missed last weekend’s squad entirely due to appendicitis, remains out of the picture as England seek to overcome the recent momentum of five successive Test losses.

sections.heading: Fiji bring back veteran experience
sections.body: Fiji have bolstered their pack with the inclusion of Peceli Yato and Levani Botia, two Top 14 veterans, as they look to recover from their opening-round loss to Wales. Interim coach Senirusi Seruvakula has emphasised the need for greater discipline, stating that while they will stick to their attacking brand of rugby, the side “must improve its discipline and decision-making” to better challenge England.

sections.heading: Vital result needed to stop slide
sections.body: England head into the fixture seeking to avoid a sixth consecutive Test defeat and a continuation of their difficult form. Following the loss in Johannesburg, the side currently sits near the bottom of the table. A win in Liverpool is seen by the coaching staff as an essential opportunity to turn their fortunes around after a challenging start to the Nations Championship.

sections.heading: Jalibert starts despite late fitness test
sections.body: France has confirmed a lineup for today's match featuring an experienced spine, including Peato Mauvaka, Emmanuel Meafou, and Romain Ntamack. Matthieu Jalibert has been selected to start at fullback, though he faces a late fitness test on a troublesome calf. Coach Fabien Galthie has maintained faith in his primary playmakers as the team looks to bounce back from last week's narrow defeat.

sections.heading: Penaud remains sidelined for tournament
sections.body: Damian Penaud is unavailable for today's Test and the remainder of the Nations Championship matches this month. The winger sustained a calf injury during the opening loss to New Zealand. There has been no fresh injury news regarding other squad members in today's coverage, suggesting the rest of the starting group remains available for selection at Suncorp Stadium.

sections.heading: Wallabies blood rookie flyhalf in Brisbane
sections.body: Australia has turned to rookie Declan Meredith at flyhalf following an injury crisis in their ranks. The Wallabies are also missing Carter Gordon and Tom Hooper for this clash. Despite these personnel struggles, Fabien Galthie has warned that the hosts remain a significant threat, noting that Australia might "blow hot and cold" while both teams remain desperate to recover from opening-round defeats.

sections.heading: Finding momentum in the middle of the pack
sections.body: France currently sits in the middle of the log after an opening defeat, having secured zero competition points. With both teams suffering two-point losses in the first round, today’s result is pivotal for establishing momentum in the tournament. A victory in Brisbane would provide a necessary reset for the side before they continue their campaign, while another loss would leave them at 0-2.

sections.heading: Farrell shuffles deck for Japanese challenge
sections.body: Andy Farrell has rotated his side significantly for today's match, making nine changes to the starting XV that edged Australia. Tadhg Beirne will captain the team for the first time, while Sean Jansen earns his first cap in the back row. Ciarán Frawley is set for his first start at out-half, guiding a new-look lineup that includes Ronan Kelleher earning his 50th cap.

sections.heading: Squad health update
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news in today's coverage regarding the traveling squad. Robert Baloucoune remains sidelined after his late withdrawal from the opening Test against Australia, and he is not included in the matchday 23 for this encounter. All other players in the 36-man touring group have been factored into the selection plans for this week's rotation.

sections.heading: Eddie Jones returns to the coaching box
sections.body: Japan coach Eddie Jones is back from his four-match ban to lead a side that features one change, with Taira Main replacing Kippei Ishida on the wing. Jones has suggested he expects Japan to dominate the Irish scrum. The Brave Blossoms arrive following a 27-10 victory over Italy, having restricted the Azzurri to a single try in a disciplined opening round performance.

sections.heading: Looking to build momentum
sections.body: Ireland enters today's match sitting fifth in the 12-team tournament table following a narrow 33-31 victory against Australia. Securing a win today is crucial for Ireland to maintain their unbeaten start to the Nations Championship before the squad travels to Auckland for next weekend’s fixture against the All Blacks. Consistency remains the primary objective for this rotated side in Newcastle.

sections.heading: Allan and Lynagh return to starting backline
sections.body: Italy have made three changes to the starting lineup following the opening defeat to Japan. Fullback Tommaso Allan and wing Louis Lynagh have been recalled to the XV to inject fresh energy into the attack. Captain Michele Lamaro continues to lead from the flank in a side featuring an otherwise settled core as they prepare for the immense challenge of facing the All Blacks in Wellington.

sections.heading: Squad status remains stable before kickoff
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news in today's coverage following the announcement of the starting XV. The coaching staff has maintained consistency in their selections, and no players have been ruled out of Saturday's Test since the team was named earlier this week. The group is set to take the field with the personnel confirmed in Thursday's final match-day squad update.

sections.heading: All Blacks name powerful side for Wellington Test
sections.body: New Zealand have confirmed a formidable lineup for their home fixture, with Codie Taylor captaining the side from hooker and Ardie Savea starting at No. 8. Notably, Anton Segner is set to become the first Germany-born player to represent the All Blacks should he appear from the bench. New Zealand enter this clash after a narrow victory over France in their tournament opener last weekend.

sections.heading: Italy seek momentum after difficult tournament start
sections.body: Following the opening round loss to Japan, Italy find themselves needing a significantly improved performance to climb the log. A result against a New Zealand side that has won each of the last four meetings between these nations would be a massive statement. Every point remains critical ahead of the upcoming Finals Weekend as Italy look to register their first win of this championship.

sections.heading: Finn Russell returns to start in Pretoria
sections.body: Finn Russell is back in the Scotland starting lineup for today’s fixture against South Africa at Loftus Versfeld. The fly-half returns following his recovery from a calf injury. Gregor Townsend has made 3 changes in total to the side that secured a win over Argentina last weekend, as Scotland look to build on their current position in the Nations Championship.

sections.heading: Fitness update regarding the touring squad
sections.body: Today’s coverage confirms that Finn Russell has recovered from the calf injury that sidelined him for the opening round of the tournament. Regarding other members of the squad, there is no further information or fresh injury news provided in today’s reports concerning the availability of players for this afternoon’s Test match at Loftus Versfeld.

sections.heading: Springboks name side for Loftus Versfeld clash
sections.body: South Africa have named Boan Venter, Johan Grobbelaar, and Wilco Louw in the front row for today. The hosts feature Handré Pollard at fly-half and have made 10 changes to their starting side following their win over England. Assistant coach Mzwandile Stick rejected the suggestion that this selection constitutes a secondary team, citing the depth of the world champions.

sections.heading: Points available at Loftus Versfeld
sections.body: Scotland arrive at this second round of the tournament after an opening victory in Argentina. With the Scots ranked fifth in the world, they face a South African side currently at the top of the global rankings. Both teams are looking to secure vital tournament points to strengthen their respective standing within the 2026 Nations Championship table.

sections.heading: Tandy makes three changes for San Juan
sections.body: Head coach Steve Tandy has made three alterations to the starting XV that defeated Fiji. Sam Costelow enters the side at fly-half, while Ellis Mee joins the back three. Louis Rees-Zammit moves to the bench for this second round fixture. These adjustments arrive as Wales look to build on their tournament-opening victory against a competitive Argentina side.

sections.heading: No fresh updates from the camp
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news in today's coverage regarding the traveling squad. Reporting remains focused on the tactical adjustments made by the coaching staff for this weekend's lineup. The squad continues its preparations in Argentina following last week's victory in Cardiff.

sections.heading: Argentina names side seeking first tournament win
sections.body: Argentina have named an experienced lineup as they look to recover from a defeat against Scotland. The hosts have made five changes to their starting team. Los Pumas have won three of their past five matches against Wales, including a 52-28 victory last November and a 29-17 win during the 2023 Rugby World Cup.

sections.heading: Testing progress against the Pumas
sections.body: Wales enter this second round fixture following a 39-24 victory against Fiji. With this match acting as the first away test of the competition, the outcome will provide further insight into the team's development. Following this encounter, the squad is scheduled to travel to South Africa for their next match.

match.venue: Estadio San Juan del Bicentenario, San Juan

sections.heading: Pumas name experienced side for bounce-back bid
sections.body: Argentina returns to the Estadio Bicentenario with a seasoned starting XV tasked with rebounding from their opening round defeat to Scotland. The selection sees a settled core looking to sharpen their attacking precision after last weekend's high-scoring encounter. Los Pumas enter this fixture aiming to leverage their forward pack and home crowd support to secure their first points of the tournament.

sections.heading: Squad status update
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news in today's coverage regarding the Argentina camp. Following the return of key forward Marcos Kremer, who missed the opening match due to personal reasons, the coaching staff has named a robust lineup for the weekend. The squad appears focused on rectifying the defensive lapses seen in the loss to Scotland as they prepare to face the Welsh.

sections.heading: Wales make three changes for San Juan clash
sections.body: Wales head coach Steve Tandy has made three changes to his starting side following their victory over Fiji. Louis Rees-Zammit moves to the bench for this contest. Adam Beard, who has previous experience winning in Argentina, returns to the pack. Wales arrive with back-to-back Test wins and, according to Tandy, the group is focused on maintaining consistency as they look to build upon their recent form.

sections.heading: Seeking a first win of the campaign
sections.body: Both nations arrive at this fixture searching for their maiden victory in the inaugural Nations Championship. While Argentina claimed a losing bonus point in their 38-47 defeat to Scotland, they currently sit in the bottom half of the log. A win today is critical for the Pumas to regain momentum before the tournament progresses deeper into its opening stages.

match.venue: Estadio San Juan del Bicentenario, San Juan

sections.heading: Looking for answers after France loss
sections.body: Following the 26-42 loss to France, the focus shifts to internal hierarchy and depth. The second half of the Brisbane match proved difficult for the Wallabies, who conceded 30 points after leading at the interval. Questions remain regarding the side's ability to maintain performance levels across the full 80 minutes after the dominant first half display.

sections.heading: Flyhalf remains unavailable
sections.body: The Wallabies are without a key playmaker for the match against Italy. This star flyhalf was previously ruled out for this fixture and the preceding test against France. No other injury news has been reported in the current coverage regarding the status of the wider squad heading into the next round of the tournament.

sections.heading: Italy next up for Australia
sections.body: Italy is the next fixture for the Wallabies in Round 3 of the Nations Championship. The team will look to recover from their recent losses as they prepare to face the Italian side. Coverage of the tournament currently focuses on ranking permutations for the remaining rounds of the competition and the results of the second round matches.

sections.heading: Seeking first tournament victory
sections.body: Australia sits with two losses from two matches in the Nations Championship. With a points difference of -18, the upcoming match against Italy provides an opportunity to secure points and improve standing on the log. A win is required to address the current deficit and move off the lower end of the table during this weekend's round of action.

sections.heading: Eddie Jones returns to lead side
sections.body: Eddie Jones is back in the coach's box for Japan after serving his four-match ban. The side shows only one change from the victory over Italy, with Taira Main entering the starting lineup on the wing. Jones has wasted no time in restarting his customary mind games, stating: "We expect to dominate the Irish scrum."

sections.heading: Squad status remains stable
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news in today's coverage regarding the Japan camp. The coaching staff has maintained continuity in their selection, with only one alteration to the starting XV following the opening round. By contrast, the opposition arrives with several players unavailable for selection, including the hamstrung Robert Baloucoune, who was a late withdrawal from their previous fixture.

sections.heading: Farrell rotates his squad
sections.body: Ireland head coach Andy Farrell has made nine changes to his starting side for this encounter in Newcastle. Tadhg Beirne captains the team for the first time as the tourists look to build on their opening win against Australia. The selection includes four uncapped players in the matchday 23, with Ciarán Frawley handed his first start at out-half alongside scrum-half Craig Casey.

sections.heading: Japan eyes second win
sections.body: Following a successful opening weekend against Italy, Japan continues its Nations Championship campaign in what is technically an away game for the Brave Blossoms. With Ireland aiming to remain unbeaten, today’s result is critical for Japan’s momentum in the tournament. A strong performance here remains the primary focus before the squad prepares to host France in Tokyo.

sections.heading: Confidence high following Italy victory
sections.body: The All Blacks move into the third round of the Nations Championship following a clinical 47-17 win against Italy. Will Jordan was the standout performer, crossing for a hat-trick of tries to become the record All Blacks try scorer. The coaching staff will now look to maintain this momentum as the squad shifts focus toward the upcoming Test against Ireland next weekend.

sections.heading: Camp availability status
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news in today's coverage regarding the New Zealand squad. The team is currently continuing its preparations for the next fixture, and there have been no official reports of new fitness concerns or player absences surfacing in the wake of the round two victory over Italy. Further updates on player availability are expected as training intensity ramps up early next week.

sections.heading: Ireland build momentum in Japan
sections.body: Ireland secured a 26-18 victory over Japan in Newcastle earlier today. The Irish were efficient in attack, with tries from Nick Timoney, Tom O’Toole, Robbie Henshaw, and debutant Sean Jansen. While the visitors overcame some struggles at the line-out, they successfully navigated the challenge from the hosts. Coach Andy Farrell will now prepare his side for their journey to New Zealand for the round three encounter.

sections.heading: Unbeaten start sets up crunch round
sections.body: New Zealand currently sits with two wins from two matches to begin the inaugural Nations Championship campaign. Having accumulated 81 points in their opening fixtures, the team holds a healthy points difference of +32. The upcoming Test against Ireland represents a significant opportunity to consolidate this position and maintain their unbeaten record at the top of the log during the tournament's opening phase.

sections.heading: Erasmus shuffles the deck for second round
sections.body: Rassie Erasmus has opted for significant rotation, making 10 changes to the starting line-up that secured a 45-21 victory over England. Only Jesse Kriel, Damian Willemse, Pieter-Steph du Toit, and Canan Moodie retain their starting spots from the opening round. Du Toit will captain the side at Loftus Versfeld, leading a refreshed unit that aims to build on last week’s emphatic performance.

sections.heading: Camp reports remain quiet ahead of kickoff
sections.body: There is no fresh injury news in today's coverage regarding the Springbok squad. While the coaching staff has utilized the depth of their roster to implement extensive changes, reports have not highlighted any fitness concerns emerging from the camp following the victory against England. The team enters this match with the players previously named in the confirmed match-day 23 expected to feature.

sections.heading: Scotland forced into late bench adjustment
sections.body: Scotland have confirmed Max Williamson will join the replacements following a knee injury to Alex Samuel. Captain Sione Tuipulotu leads a side that impressed in their 47-38 win over Argentina. Coach Gregor Townsend acknowledges the scale of the task, stating that the Springboks are "the biggest challenge in world rugby" as his team looks to improve on a recent record that includes three straight defeats against South Africa.

sections.heading: Ranking leaders test momentum in Pretoria
sections.body: The Springboks head into today’s encounter ranked number 1 in the world, aiming to consolidate their position after an opening victory. For Scotland, a win in Pretoria would represent a major statement against the world champions. With both teams victorious last week, the result of this fixture will be pivotal in determining the early momentum in the Nations Championship table.
